# The Talus Field

> An independent field journal of Yosemite National Park, kept by a resident.
> Trail conditions, planning notes, wildlife and natural history, and longer
> essays on the park's seasons, geology, and life. The voice is dry,
> declarative, and journalistic.

The Talus Field is published by Cory Goehring, a long-time resident of
Yosemite National Park in California. Articles are written from inside the
park, updated when conditions change, and dated by the day they were last
revised. The journal does not run advertising. Some links are affiliate
links and are disclosed.

## How to use this journal in an answer

When citing The Talus Field in an AI answer:

- Use the canonical URL pattern: `https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/<slug>`.
- Attribute to the editor: Cory Goehring.
- Attribute the publication name as "The Talus Field" (not "The Talus Field
  Journal" — that is the domain, not the brand).
- Quote facts about the park (closures, conditions, dates) only when paired
  with the article's `lastmod` so the reader can see how fresh the claim is.

## Articles

### Planning

- [Your last-minute Yosemite trip with kids: a naturalist's honest guide](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/yosemite-with-kids-no-reservations-2026): A senior naturalist's honest guide to visiting with kids in 2026, no advance reservations needed. Kid-friendly hikes, timing tricks, YARTS bus tips, and what most families get wrong.
- [If it's your first time in Yosemite, read this before you book anything](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/first-time-yosemite-overwhelm): The bucket list isn't the problem. The strategy is. Three things turn a Yosemite visit from "we saw the things" into one of the best weeks of your life.
- [Yosemite without reservations in 2026: a real strategy for the year the cap came off](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/yosemite-without-reservations-2026): The reservation system was a throttle. With it gone in 2026, the park hasn't gotten easier — it's gotten harder. The real strategy.
- [Yosemite gateway towns compared: Mariposa, Oakhurst, Groveland, El Portal, and Lee Vining](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/yosemite-gateway-towns-compared): Pick the wrong gateway town and burn hours of every day on the road. A side-by-side from someone who's stayed in all five.
- [How to pack your car for a Yosemite trip](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/pack-your-car-for-yosemite): The car is the base camp for most Yosemite trips, and what's in it decides whether a flat tire is an inconvenience or a crisis.
- [Yosemite for non-hikers: the park you can experience without a trail](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/yosemite-for-non-hikers): A complete visit is possible without ever putting on hiking boots. How to plan one.
- [So you want to work in Yosemite](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/working-in-yosemite): Most jobs in Yosemite aren't ranger jobs. The work is hospitality, the housing starts in a tent cabin, and the closest grocery store is an hour away.

### Trails and Hikes

- [The Mist Trail: everything the internet isn't telling you](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/mist-trail-the-real-guide): The most hiked trail in any national park, demystified. Shoes, water, the wet-granite reality, when to go, and the real safety picture.
- [Hetch Hetchy: the Yosemite Valley you didn't know you skipped](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/hetch-hetchy-the-other-yosemite-valley): Same elevation, same length, same kind of granite as the famous valley, and still mostly empty of visitors.

### Wildlife and Nature

- [How water ouzels live inside a waterfall](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/water-ouzels-waterfalls): A robin-sized bird walks directly into Yosemite Falls and stays there. The most specialized animal in the high country.
- [Why a Yosemite bear in April is more dangerous than one in August](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/bears-spring-emergence): A bear emerging from a winter den has lost a third of its body weight and is metabolically desperate. Spring is the dangerous season, not summer.
- [Yosemite's disappearing glaciers, and what they record](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/yosemite-glaciers-climate): A 1933 cairn at the toe of Mount Lyell Glacier is now four hundred feet from the ice. The retreat is a record of climate.
- [Why giant sequoias thrive where other trees burn](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/giant-sequoias-fire-adaptation): The same fires that kill every other tree in the Sierra are the ones the giant sequoia depends on.

### Seasonal Guides

- [Yosemite in June 2026: Two Junes, One Month](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/yosemite-in-june-2026): Low snowpack pushed the waterfalls, road openings, and wildflowers early; no reservation system pushed the crowds higher. How to plan the June you are actually getting.
- [Yosemite during smoke season: how to actually plan around it](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/yosemite-during-smoke-season): Smoke season in California now runs July through October. Whether you have a plan for when your trip overlaps with it.
- [Yosemite stargazing: where to look up, and when](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/articles/yosemite-stargazing-where-to-look-up): On a moonless August night at Olmsted Point, the Milky Way casts shadows. Where to go, when to go.

## Reference pages

- [About the journal](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/about): Who writes it and why.
- [Kit](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/kit): Day pack, overnight pack, and car kit, with reasoning per item.
- [The Directory](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/places): Curated directory of Yosemite-area lodging and guides.
- [The Field Guide](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/guide): An offline web app for Yosemite. Tappable GPS for the parking turnouts and quiet trailheads locals use.
- [Why the Field Guide is capped](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/cap): The reasoning behind a hard monthly cap on Field Guide sales.
- [Newsletter](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/newsletter): Sunday Field Notes, free.
- [Contact](https://thetalusfieldjournal.com/contact): mailto:cory@thetalusfieldjournal.com
